description: You are a labor economist analyzing the causal return to education on wages. Using the 'card' dataset from the 'wooldridge' package, replicate the classic Instrumental Variables (IV) analysis using proximity to a 4-year college ('nearc4') as an instrument for education ('educ'). Compare an OLS model and a 2SLS (IV) model for log(wage) using educ, exper, exper^2, black, smsa, and south as predictors. Document the comparison in a CSV file at /home/ga/RProjects/output/wage_analysis_comparison.csv with columns 'model' (OLS, IV), 'estimate_educ', and 'std_error_educ'. Additionally, perform a Wu-Hausman test for endogeneity, saving the result to /home/ga/RProjects/output/hausman_test_result.txt, and generate a boxplot of 'nearc4' versus 'educ' saved to /home/ga/RProjects/output/first_stage_instrument.png. Save the complete analysis script to /home/ga/RProjects/returns_to_schooling.R.
